ULF-Synth
  • Python API

Python API

ULF-Synth: Physics-Guided Ultra-Low-Field MRI Enhancement & Simulation.

Submodules:

simulate — Synthesize realistic ULF MRI from high-field NIfTI volumes enhance — Enhance ULF MRI using pretrained restoration models cli — Command-line interface (ulfsynth simulate, ulfsynth enhance)

—

ulfsynth.simulate

Physics-guided ULF MRI simulation from high-field volumes.

If you use ULF-Synth in your work, please cite:

Musah et al. “ULF-Synth: Physics-Guided Ultra-Low-Field MRI Enhancement for Pediatric Neuroimaging.” arXiv:2605.24625, 2026.

ulfsynth.simulate.apply_b0_inhomogeneity(K, distortion_strength, smoothness)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.apply_b0_t2star_decay(I_complex, T2, TE, b0_strength, smoothness, k, eps=1e-06)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.kspace_crop(K, crop_ratio)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.noisy_kspace(K, I_ref, signal_target)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.object_mask(vol, thresh=0.05)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.sample_params(rng=None)[source]

Sample random simulation parameters.

Returns a dictionary of physics parameters that control the ULF degradation pipeline. Each call draws fresh random values from pre-defined distributions that model realistic 0.064 T acquisitions.

Parameters:

rng – Optional random number generator (numpy.random or numpy.random.Generator). Defaults to numpy.random.

Returns:

dict with keys T2, TE, b0_strength, smoothness, k, signal_target, crop_ratio, acceleration, center_fraction.

ulfsynth.simulate.simulate_file(hf_path, out_path, seed=None, verbose=True)[source]

Simulate ULF from a single file and save the result.

Parameters:

  • hf_path – Input high-field NIfTI path.

  • out_path – Output ULF NIfTI path.

  • seed – Random seed (None = non-deterministic).

  • verbose – Print a summary line when done.

Returns:

The parameter dict used for this simulation.

ulfsynth.simulate.simulate_folder(in_dir, out_dir, seed=None, verbose=True)[source]

Simulate ULF for every NIfTI file in a folder.

Parameters:

  • in_dir – Directory containing input high-field NIfTI files.

  • out_dir – Directory for output ULF NIfTI files (created if needed).

  • seed – Base random seed. Each file gets a deterministic offset derived from its filename.

  • verbose – Print a summary line per file.

Returns:

List of parameter dicts, one per file.

ulfsynth.simulate.simulate_ulf(hf_path, seed=None, params=None)[source]

Synthesize a ULF volume from a high-field NIfTI file.

Applies the full physics-guided degradation pipeline: polarisation scaling, T2* decay with B0 inhomogeneity, thermal noise, k-space cropping, undersampling, and off-resonance distortion.

Parameters:

  • hf_path – Path to the input high-field NIfTI file.

  • seed – Random seed for reproducibility. None = non-deterministic.

  • params – Parameter dict (see sample_params()). None = sample fresh parameters.

Returns:

Tuple of (ulf_volume, affine, header, params) where ulf_volume is a (H, W, D) float32 array, affine and header are copied from the input, and params is the parameter dict used.

ulfsynth.simulate.to_complex_ispace(K)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.to_ispace(K)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.to_kspace(vol)[source]
ulfsynth.simulate.undersample_kspace(K, acceleration, center_fraction)[source]

ulfsynth.enhance

Enhance ULF MRI volumes using pretrained restoration models.

If you use ULF-Synth enhancement in your work, please cite:

Musah et al. “ULF-Synth: Physics-Guided Ultra-Low-Field MRI Enhancement for Pediatric Neuroimaging.” arXiv:2605.24625, 2026.

ulfsynth.enhance.enhance_file(input_path, output_path, device='cuda', verbose=True)[source]
ulfsynth.enhance.enhance_folder(in_dir, out_dir, device='cuda', verbose=True)[source]

ulfsynth.cli

Command-line interface for ULF-Synth.

Usage:

ulfsynth simulate input output [–seed N] [–quiet] ulfsynth enhance input output [–device DEVICE] [–quiet] ulfsynth download-weights [–force]

ulfsynth.cli._run_download(args)[source]
ulfsynth.cli._run_enhance(args)[source]
ulfsynth.cli._run_simulate(args)[source]
ulfsynth.cli.add_download_parser(subparsers)[source]
ulfsynth.cli.add_enhance_parser(subparsers)[source]
ulfsynth.cli.add_simulate_parser(subparsers)[source]
ulfsynth.cli.main()[source]
